title: Writing - Part 1: Renewable Energy
description: Your class has been discussing the importance of switching to renewable energy sources and the best ways to encourage this transition.

content.question: Write an essay discussing two of the methods in your notes. You should explain which method you think is most effective, giving reasons for your opinion.

opinions: Financial support from governments can make renewable energy more affordable for households.
People will only change their habits if they understand the urgency of the climate crisis.
Without firm legal deadlines, the switch to clean energy will be too slow.

structure.intro: Introduce the need for renewable energy and state the two methods you will discuss.
structure.body1: Discuss the first method and explain its potential impact.
structure.body2: Discuss the second method and compare its effectiveness to the first.
structure.conclusion: Summarise your discussion and state your recommended approach.

content.modelAnswer:
The transition to renewable energy is widely regarded as essential if we are to combat climate change effectively. Two key methods for accelerating this shift are government subsidies and public awareness campaigns.

Government subsidies can make renewable energy technologies such as solar panels and wind turbines financially accessible. When households and businesses receive support to cover installation costs, adoption increases significantly. Several countries have demonstrated that subsidies can drive rapid growth in clean energy use, reducing reliance on fossil fuels within just a few years.

Public awareness campaigns, meanwhile, aim to change attitudes and behaviour. By educating people about the environmental damage caused by fossil fuels and the benefits of renewables, campaigns can build the public support necessary for meaningful change. An informed and motivated population is essential to any successful energy transition.

In my opinion, government subsidies are the more effective method. While awareness is important, financial barriers are often the primary reason people fail to adopt renewable energy. Subsidies remove these barriers directly and produce measurable results. With strong government investment, the transition to cleaner energy could happen far more quickly.
